meta.title: The Essence-Energies Distinction: A Protestant Reflection
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: Who does the speaker present as a pinnacle figure of the Eastern (Palamite) tradition on essence and energies?
questions.answer: Gregory Palamas

questions.question: The Palamite distinction between God's essence and energies is most often understood, in the presentation given, as:
options: A real distinction in God, not merely conceptual or formal
A purely linguistic convention with no basis in how God is in himself
A distinction that only applies after God creates the world
A distinction invented in the Reformation
questions.answer: A real distinction in God, not merely conceptual or formal

questions.question: Which practice is central to Hesychasm as described in the video?
questions.answer: The Jesus Prayer

questions.question: Gregory Palamas's theological opponent in the controversy discussed is called:
questions.answer: Barlaam

questions.question: Councils held in which city in 1341 sided with Gregory Palamas?
questions.answer: Constantinople

questions.question: David Bradshaw is cited for the claim that the essence–energy distinction is:
options: The most important philosophical tenet distinguishing Eastern from Western Christian thought
Rejected by all contemporary Orthodox scholars
A minor liturgical detail in Eastern Christianity
questions.answer: The most important philosophical tenet distinguishing Eastern from Western Christian thought

questions.question: The common metaphor Gregory Palamas uses for essence and energies compares them to:
options: The sun and the sun's rays
questions.answer: The sun and the sun's rays

questions.question: On divine simplicity, the speaker describes the Western (Thomist) tendency as affirming that:
options: God's essence is composed of many separable parts
God's essence is identical with his attributes
questions.answer: God's essence is identical with his attributes

questions.question: The uncreated light associated with Mount Athos is often linked biblically to which event?
options: The crossing of the Red Sea
questions.answer: The Transfiguration on Mount Tabor

questions.question: According to the speaker's summary, the Palamite tradition typically says the blessed see in the beatific vision:
options: Only symbolic visions with no real knowledge of God
questions.answer: God's energies, not God's essence

questions.question: The speaker says Thomas Aquinas holds that in heaven the blessed:
options: See God's essence, though not comprehensively
questions.answer: See God's essence, though not comprehensively

questions.question: Gregory Palamas uses the term for the divine energies that indicates they do not subsist on their own apart from God—i.e. they are:
options: Anhypostatic in the same sense as Christ's human nature before the union
questions.answer: Enhypostatic

questions.question: John Meyendorff is quoted summarizing the essence–energy distinction as a way of saying that God:
options: Has no real relation to creatures
Remains transcendent as he communicates himself to humanity
Becomes less transcendent when he creates
questions.answer: Remains transcendent as he communicates himself to humanity

questions.question: Which Western monk does the speaker compare to Palamas regarding the link between spirituality and theology?
questions.answer: Anselm of Canterbury

questions.question: The speaker's concern labeled 'diotheism' refers to the worry that essence and energies might imply:
options: Something like two principles or 'two-ness' in God
That the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Son
That there is only one person in the Trinity
questions.answer: Something like two principles or 'two-ness' in God

questions.question: On the speaker's third main worry—divine simplicity—after reading Gregory charitably, he reports that:
options: He fully accepts Palamite simplicity without reservation
The concern about simplicity remains on the table for him
He denies that Western theology ever taught simplicity
He concludes Gregory ignores simplicity entirely
questions.answer: The concern about simplicity remains on the table for him

questions.question: The speaker criticizes some inferences in the Triads for seeming to assume reasoning about God that is:
options: Univocal in a way that treats God and creatures too similarly
Entirely analogical without any logical structure
questions.answer: Univocal in a way that treats God and creatures too similarly

questions.question: In Summa Theologiae I, question 8, Thomas is cited as teaching that a thing is wherever it:
questions.answer: Operates

questions.question: The speaker uses Pascal's memorial to emphasize that God is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—not merely:
options: The God of the covenant only
The God of the Gentiles only
The God of the philosophers and scholars
The God of the law only
questions.answer: The God of the philosophers and scholars

questions.question: David Bradshaw's single-word contrast between East and West (as cited) is:
questions.answer: Synergy

questions.question: The speaker argues that Augustine's soteriological framework includes language of:
options: Deification/participation in God tied to the incarnation
Exclusive focus on forensic justification without union
questions.answer: Deification/participation in God tied to the incarnation

questions.question: Maximus the Confessor is mentioned in connection with a reading of the Transfiguration that stresses:
options: That Moses and Elijah were divine
That the disciples were enabled to see who Christ really is
That the event was purely symbolic
questions.answer: That the disciples were enabled to see who Christ really is

questions.question: The speaker describes his overall ecumenical judgment that Palamite and Thomist traditions are:
options: Both within Christian orthodoxy, despite real disagreements
questions.answer: Both within Christian orthodoxy, despite real disagreements

questions.question: Gregory Palamas is said to have spent much of his monastic life in prayer on:
questions.answer: Mount Athos

questions.question: The speaker finds problematic Bradshaw's closing linkage between Western theological errors and:
options: A wide sweep of Western history including violence and secularization
questions.answer: A wide sweep of Western history including violence and secularization
